p.em: Analysis of the transaction commit path bottleneck in distributed systems.

ul.li: Magnitude Delta: Transitioning from write() to fsync() introduces a 1000x to 10,000x increase in latency.
Throughput Optimization: High-performance databases utilize Group Commit (batching multiple logical commits into a single physical fsync) to amortize the I/O cost.
Write-Ahead Log (WAL): The critical path only flushes the WAL sequentially. Data-file flushes are performed lazily in the background to hide latency.

g.title: Memory-to-memory copy: App buffer to Kernel page cache
Dirty page residency in physical RAM
Thread context switch to kernel; initiation of I/O barrier
NAND Flash Program (SSD) or Magnetic Platter Write (HDD)
Interrupt received; system call returns to User space
